key_results.description: Close 20 new enterprise deals with ACV > $100K
Reduce sales cycle from 82 to 55 days
Ship AI workflow builder to GA with 99.5% uptime
Reduce average API response time to under 200ms
Pass SOC 2 Type II certification for enterprise readiness
Launch public API with 50+ integration partners
Grow engineering team from 18 to 32 engineers
Reduce critical tech debt items from 42 to 15
Improve employee engagement score from 72 to 85

check_in.cascade_narrative: The AI Workflow Engine (P5) hit unexpected ML inference latency issues — model response times are 800ms vs. the 200ms target. This blocks the AI Beta Program (P8) from launching on schedule, which compresses the timeline for onboarding 100 beta customers (KR2.2), and puts the entire Product objective (O2) at risk.

items.title: Slip AI Beta Program start date by 6 weeks
items.summary: The AI Workflow Engine (P5) hit ML inference latency issues — model response times are 800ms, need to be under 200ms for real-time workflows. The root cause is the data platform migration (P7) taking longer than planned. The AI Beta Program (P8) cannot launch until P5 delivers a stable build.
items.rationale: Samantha Lee's team identified the latency bottleneck in the inference pipeline. Andrew Chen estimates 3 more weeks for the data platform schema migration. David Park (PM) agrees April 15 is a realistic new target.
items.impact: KR2.2 (onboard 100 beta customers by Sept 30) timeline compressed from 7 months to 5.5 months. Still achievable if P5 stabilizes by April 15, but leaves no buffer. If P5 slips further, KR2.2 becomes unachievable.

items.title: Reallocate Carlos Mendez to focus on critical ML work
items.summary: Carlos Mendez is currently assigned to 5 projects at 120% utilization and is burning out. VP Engineering wants to pull him from P11 (API Performance) and P14 (Public API & SDK) to focus exclusively on P5 (AI Workflow Engine) and P25 (AI Model Training Pipeline) — the two most critical ML projects.
items.rationale: Carlos is the only ML engineer currently on staff. His expertise is bottlenecking P5 progress. Sarah Chen (R17) is at 20% utilization with relevant engineering skills and can backfill on P14.
items.impact: P11 loses its ML expertise — Ryan Nguyen and Andrew Chen remain but may need 3 extra weeks. P14 gains Sarah Chen who can handle SDK development. Carlos drops from 120% to ~80% utilization, reducing burnout risk.

items.title: Increase ML engineer salary band by 15%
items.summary: The Engineering Hiring Sprint (P18) has only placed 2 of 14 target engineers. The ML specialist market is extremely competitive — Novacraft lost 2 final-round candidates to Google and Meta compensation packages in the last 3 weeks.
items.rationale: Hannah Barnes: current band of $180K-$220K is below market for ML specialists. Increasing to $210K-$260K aligns with 75th percentile comp data. 3 offers are out and 2 candidates are in final rounds — the higher band could close them.
items.impact: ~$200K additional annual spend across 5 ML hires. Necessary to unblock KR3.1 (grow engineering team from 18 to 32). Without this, ML hiring stalls and P5/P25 timelines extend further. Runway impact: reduces remaining runway by ~0.5 months.

items.title: Accept SOC 2 certification delay to August
items.summary: The external auditor (Big4 firm) has a scheduling conflict. The originally planned June audit window is no longer available. The next available window is August.
items.rationale: Brandon Wright explored alternative auditors but switching mid-process would require re-starting several control documentation efforts, adding 2-3 months. Accepting the August window is the faster path.
items.impact: One month delay on SOC 2 certification. Some enterprise beta customers (KR2.2) may require interim compliance documentation (letter of intent + penetration test results) to proceed.

items.title: Accelerate Onboarding Redesign — ship 30 days early
items.summary: The Enterprise Onboarding Redesign (P17) is ahead of schedule. Jonathan Bailey's team has the new onboarding flow in pilot with 3 enterprise customers, and early results are exceptional: time-to-value reduced from 6 weeks to 3.5 weeks.
items.rationale: All 3 pilot customers gave positive feedback. The implementation is stable and ready for general availability. Shipping early means new beta customers (KR2.2) benefit from the improved onboarding from day one.
items.impact: Positive cascade: faster onboarding accelerates KR2.2 (beta customer activation) and KR2.3 (NPS improvement from reduced onboarding friction). Jonathan's team can be redeployed to support the upcoming AI Beta Program launch.

items.ai_rationale: P5 is 45% complete with 5 months remaining, but it depends on P7 (Data Platform) which is only 50% done. Carlos Mendez is on this project at 120% total utilization across 5 projects.
items.leader_narrative: Hit ML inference latency issues — 800ms response times vs. 200ms target. Root cause is the P7 schema migration. If latency isn't resolved by mid-April, the AI Beta Program (P8) can't launch on schedule.

items.ai_rationale: Carlos Mendez is assigned to 5 projects (P5, P7, P11, P14, P25) at 120% utilization. This is the highest overallocation on the team. Sarah Chen is at 20% utilization with 0 active projects.
items.leader_narrative: Carlos needs to focus on P5 and P25 — the two critical ML projects. Sarah has relevant engineering skills for P14 (SDK development).

items.ai_rationale: P12 is 40% complete with target date July 15. Progress is on track, but no auditor milestone is scheduled.
items.leader_narrative: Project itself is on-track. External auditor (Big4) has a scheduling conflict — June audit window no longer available. Next window is August. Switching auditors mid-process would add 2-3 months. Accepting the August window is faster.

frictions.description: Carlos Mendez removal from P11 impacts KR2.4 (API Latency), which is owned by VP Product. VP Product was not consulted. AI auto-tagged VP Product for review.

P22 (Tech Debt Sprint) has had no progress in 3 weeks. Samantha Lee (lead) has been pulled into P5 firefighting full-time. The 12% figure is stale. VP Engineering confirmed it as On Track without updating.

items.ai_rationale: 3 deals closed out of 20 target (15% at month 2 of 12). Linear pace would suggest ~3.3 by now. On track.
items.leader_narrative: Closed FinServ deal this week, $140K ACV. Pipeline is strong with 5 more in late-stage.

items.ai_rationale: Sales cycle at 72 days vs. 55-day target. Only 10-day improvement in 2 months. At this rate, you'll reach ~60 days by September, missing the target.
items.leader_narrative: The 72-day number is skewed by two legacy deals in the pipeline. New deals using the updated playbook are averaging 58 days. Expect the metric to improve as legacy deals close out.

items.ai_rationale: 75% complete, end date March 31. On pace.
items.leader_narrative: Playbook complete, training sessions start next week.

frictions.description: VP Sales marked KR1.2 as On Track, but the metric (72 days) is trending off-pace. VP Sales provided a rationale about legacy deals skewing the number — facilitator must judge whether to trust the data or the leader's context.

P2 (Demo Portal) needs real AI workflow demos, which depend on P5 (AI Workflow Engine). If P5 is delayed, the demo portal ships with placeholder content. No formal dependency was recorded. This will surface as a surprise in 2 months.

items.ai_rationale: This KR depends on P5 (AI Workflow Engine) which is At Risk, and P6 (Workflow Builder UI) at 35%. Target date June 30 is tight.
items.leader_narrative: P6 is on track independently — the risk is entirely from P5.

items.ai_rationale: P8 was scheduled to start March 1 but depends on P5 delivering a stable build. P5 is At Risk.
items.leader_narrative: We can launch a 'lite beta' with the 3 stable workflow templates that work today, without the full ML inference pipeline. This gets us customer feedback 4 weeks earlier while P5 catches up. Lite beta covers ~40% of the planned feature set. Beta NPS will be lower. But waiting 6 more weeks for perfection risks the entire KR2.2 target.

items.ai_rationale: Early stages, target Dec 31. No beta launched yet but timeline allows ramp-up.
items.leader_narrative: If P5 slips beyond April 15, the 100-customer target becomes unachievable regardless of beta approach. We'd need to either reduce the target to 60 or extend the deadline to Q4.

items.ai_rationale: NPS at 36, up from 32 baseline. Target 50. Progress is gradual but trending right.
items.leader_narrative: Latest survey came back at 38 (up from 36). Top 3 detractor themes: onboarding complexity, missing API docs, slow support response.

items.ai_rationale: 14% progress, target Sept 30. 3 offers out, 2 in final rounds, but lost 2 candidates recently.

Will keep losing candidates without adjustment. 3 offers are out now — the higher band could close them. Without this, P5 and P25 timelines extend further. VP Finance has concerns — recommend we discuss tradeoffs: either raise the band, bring on ML contractors at higher hourly rates, or accept that P5/P25 will be slower.

frictions.description: P17 completion frees 3 team members (Jonathan Bailey, Patrick Gray, Vanessa James). Neither VP CS nor VP Product has proposed where to reassign them. AI suggests reallocation to P16 or P8.

P16 is technically on track but has lost momentum. Christina Rivera has been pulled into ad-hoc customer escalations not tracked in the plan. VP CS frames it gently without quantifying the escalation drain.
